content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

March 6, 1990

Gordon Cologne
Gordon Cologne & Associates
925 L Street, Suite 1150
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-109

Dear Mr. Cologne:

You have requested advice concerning the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTION

Should The Doctors’ Company or its wholly-owned subsidiary, The Doctors’ Management Company (The Management Company), be listed as the lobbyist employer on your lobbying firm’s registration statement and reporting statements?

CONCLUSION

Since it is The Management Company that reports to the Secretary of State as a lobbyist employer, and it is The Management Company that makes payments to you for your lobbying services, you should list The Management Company as your employer on your lobbying firm’s registration and reporting statements.

FACTS

You are a registered lobbying firm. You report The Doctors’ Company on your firm’s quarterly statements and have registered The Doctors’ Company as your firm’s employer. However, it is The Management Company through which you receive payments for your lobbying services. In addition, according to Sheldon Davidow, Vice President of Governmental and Corporate Relations for The Doctors’ Company, The Management Company files quarterly statements as a lobbyist employer. The Doctors’ Company does not file reporting statements.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

The Management Company is a wholly owned subsidiary of The Doctors' Company.

ANALYSIS

Section 82039.5 defines a "lobbyist employer," in part, as any person who ... contracts for the services of a lobbying firm for economic consideration ... for the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.

Since it is The Management Company that makes payments to you and is the managing arm of The Doctors' Company, it appears that your contract is with The Management Company. Thus, The Doctors' Management Company should be reported as the lobbyist employer on your lobbying firm's registration and reporting statements. Because your lobbying firm has registered The Doctors' Company as its employer, the Secretary of State's office expects to receive quarterly lobbyist employer reports from The Doctors' Company. Confusion arises since The Doctor's Company does not file these reports.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant
